# Redrain

> Unofficial Ruby client for Rain's Issuing API (card issuing: applications,
> users, companies, cards, transactions, disputes, payments, signatures).
> Port of the official Python SDK `rain-sdk`. Covers all 60 endpoints of
> Issuing API v1.2.1. No runtime dependencies beyond stdlib.

Not affiliated with Signify Holdings, Inc. "Rain" is their trademark.

## When to use this gem

- The host application needs to issue or manage Rain cards from Ruby.
- Anything the Python `rain-sdk` does; method names and nesting match it
  one-for-one, so its `api.md` doubles as documentation for this gem.

## Install

```ruby
gem "redrain"
```

No other gems required.

## Client construction

```ruby
rain = Redrain::Client.new(
  api_key: ENV.fetch("RAIN_API_KEY"),  # falls back to RAIN_API_KEY
  environment: :production,            # :dev (DEFAULT) or :production
  timeout: 60,                         # read timeout, seconds
  open_timeout: 5,                     # connect timeout, seconds
  max_retries: 2,                      # 0 disables retrying
  default_headers: {}
)
```

| Setting | Default | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `environment:` | `:dev` | **Defaults to dev, not production.** Set it explicitly. Only `:dev` and `:production` are valid. |
| `base_url:` | derived | Overrides `environment:`. Also readable from `RAIN_BASE_URL`. |
| `max_retries:` | 2 | Retries 408/409/429/5xx and connection failures only. |

Base URL precedence, highest first: `base_url:` > `RAIN_BASE_URL` > `environment:`.
The environment is validated even when a URL overrides it, so a typo like
`environment: :prod` raises `Redrain::ConfigurationError` immediately.

## Resource tree

`client.<resource>.<method>`, nesting exactly as the Python SDK does:

- `applications.company` — `create` `retrieve` `update` `reapply` `upload_document`
- `applications.company.ubo` — `update` `upload_document`
- `applications.company.ubo.document` — `upload`
- `applications.user` — `create` `retrieve` `update` `initiate` `reapply` `upload_document`
- `balances` — `retrieve`
- `cards` — `retrieve` `update` `list` `retrieve_secrets`
- `cards.pin` — `retrieve` `update`
- `companies` — `retrieve` `update` `list` `charge` `create_user` `initiate_payment` `retrieve_balances` `retrieve_contracts`
- `companies.signatures` — `retrieve_payment_signature` `retrieve_withdrawal_signature`
- `contracts` — `list`
- `disputes` — `retrieve` `update` `list`
- `disputes.evidence` — `list` (binary) `upload`
- `keys` — `create` `delete`
- `payments` — `initiate`
- `signatures` — `retrieve_payment_signature` `retrieve_withdrawal_signature`
- `transactions` — `retrieve` `update` `list` `create_dispute`
- `transactions.receipt` — `retrieve` (binary) `upload`
- `users` — `create` `retrieve` `update` `list` `delete` `create_card` `create_charge` `initiate_payment` `retrieve_balances` `retrieve_contracts`
- `users.signatures` — `retrieve_payment_signature` `retrieve_withdrawal_signature`

## Calling convention

The resource id nearest the endpoint is positional; everything else is a
keyword. Required keywords have no default; optional ones default to `nil` and
are omitted from the request entirely.

```ruby
rain.users.create(email: "a@b.com", first_name: "Ada", last_name: "Lovelace")
rain.users.retrieve(user_id)
rain.cards.pin.update(card_id, encrypted_pin_block: "...")
rain.applications.company.ubo.update(ubo_id, company_id: company_id, first_name: "Ada")
```

Parameters are snake_case in Ruby and camelCase on the wire; the gem maps them.
Never pass camelCase.

## Responses

Typed objects with snake_case readers. Nested objects and arrays are typed too;
`date-time` fields come back as `Time`.

```ruby
card = rain.cards.retrieve(id)
card.status            # "active"
card.limit.amount      # nested model
card["newFieldRainJustAdded"]   # unknown keys are preserved, read by wire name
card.to_h              # raw body as Rain sent it
card.to_snake_h        # snake_case keys, coerced values
```

`IssuingTransaction` (`type`: spend/collateral/payment/fee) and
`IssuingSignature` (`status`: pending/ready) are unions flattened into one class
with predicates — `transaction.spend?`, `signature.ready?`. Only the matching
detail field is populated; the others are `nil`.

204 responses return `nil`. The two `application/octet-stream` endpoints
(`disputes.evidence.list`, `transactions.receipt.retrieve`) return a binary
`String`.

## Pagination

List endpoints are cursor-paginated (`cursor:`, `limit:`) and return an Array.
Prefer the pager over hand-rolled cursor loops:

```ruby
rain.transactions.auto_paging_each(user_id: id) { |txn| ... }
rain.cards.auto_paging_each.lazy.select { |c| c.status == "active" }.first(5)
```

Available on `cards`, `companies`, `disputes`, `transactions`, `users`.

## File uploads

```ruby
rain.applications.user.upload_document(id, document: File.open("id.png"), type: "idCard", side: "front")
rain.disputes.evidence.upload(id, name: "Receipt", type: "receipt", evidence: Pathname("r.pdf"))
```

`File`, `Pathname`, any `IO`, or a raw byte `String` all work. When filename or
content type matter and can't be inferred, use
`Redrain::Upload.new(bytes, filename:, content_type:)`. Rain caps uploads at 20 MB.

## Errors

All under `Redrain::Error`. `Redrain::APIStatusError` (and its per-status
subclasses `BadRequestError`, `AuthenticationError`, `PermissionDeniedError`,
`NotFoundError`, `ConflictError`, `UnprocessableEntityError`, `RateLimitError`,
`InternalServerError`) carries `#status`, `#body`, `#headers`, `#request_id`,
`#error_message`. Transport failures raise `APIConnectionError` /
`APITimeoutError`. Local misconfiguration raises `ConfigurationError`.

Rain's error bodies have no schema — treat `#body` as opaque and log
`#request_id` for support.

## Behaviours an agent should NOT change

1. **Never hand-edit `lib/redrain/models/` or `lib/redrain/resources/`.** They
   are generated. Fix `dev/generate.rb` and run `rake generate`.
2. Don't drop the `environment:` argument in production code — the default is
   `:dev` and a missing argument silently talks to the wrong API.
3. Don't add runtime gem dependencies. The gem is stdlib-only on purpose.
4. Don't send camelCase keys or `null` for "unset" — pass `nil` and let the
   client omit the key.
5. Don't retry 4xx yourself; the client already retries what is safe to retry.

## Common pitfalls

- `Redrain::Client.new` with no arguments points at **dev**.
- Card secrets and PINs come back encrypted (`encrypted_pan`, `encrypted_pin`);
  decrypting them is the host application's job, not this gem's.
- `applications.company.ubo.upload_document` identifies the UBO by `email:`,
  while `applications.company.ubo.document.upload` takes a `ubo_id` positionally.
  They are different endpoints.
- List endpoints return bare Arrays, not a page object — there is no `total`.
- `rake generate` fails loudly if `dev/resource_map.yml` and the vendored spec
  disagree about routes. That's the drift alarm; reconcile, don't bypass.

## Regenerating after a Rain API bump

```sh
rake sync_spec   # re-fetch the spec, report added/removed routes and schemas
# adopt openapi/rain-issuing.json, update openapi/stats.yml,
# reconcile dev/resource_map.yml
rake generate
rake test
```

## Resources

- Repository:  https://github.com/julik/redrain
- Port plan:   https://github.com/julik/redrain/blob/main/llm/plans/port-plan.md
- Upstream SDK + method map: https://github.com/SignifyHQ/rain-sdk-python/blob/main/api.md
- Vendored spec: `openapi/rain-issuing.json` (Issuing API v1.2.1)
